Empires & Puzzles Lazara Review: The Baseball Champion

Step up to the plate with Lazara, the baseball-swinging Orc who cleanses allies without triggering nasty secondary effects. Is she still a home run hitter in today's meta, or has her batting average dropped? Read our deep dive review to find out.
Lazara Review: The Grand Slam Champion
Lazara, The Grand Slam Champion: Cleansing, Crushing, and Home Runs

Lazara is a 5-star Holy hero from the Champions family, originally hitting the field in March 2024. She brings a unique "baseball" theme to a world of swords and sorcery, proving that sometimes a good old-fashioned bat is mightier than a magic staff. Her primary role is that of a "Safe Cleanser"—a specialized support hero designed to counter the nasty trend of status ailments that punish you for removing them. While she can hit hard, her true value lies in her utility and her ability to turn the enemy's cleansing attempts against them.

Lazara stands out with one of the most distinct visual themes in the game. She's a green-skinned Orc geared up in full baseball attire—helmet, pads, and a massive wooden bat slung over her shoulder. The art is dynamic, capturing her mid-swing or ready to strike, with a baseball flying in the background. It's a playful, sporty aesthetic that breaks the mold of high fantasy, and her special skill animation—literally hitting a home run—is widely considered one of the most satisfying in the game.
Lazara's stats reflect her release era (early 2024). With a Limit Break 2 (LB2) Attack of roughly
, she falls significantly behind modern powerhouse heroes who push 1600+ in primary stats. Her HP of

is also low compared to the 2800+ behemoths released in late 2025. While she was once a stat monster, she now sits firmly in the "average" to "below average" category for raw numbers. However, her utility often outweighs her raw stats.
This skill is the reason you draft Lazara onto your team.
The phrase "without triggering secondary effects" is the MVP here. In a meta filled with heroes like
—who deal massive damage if you cleanse their ailments—Lazara is the hard counter. She wipes the slate clean without triggering the punishment.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Champions family is all about aggression and punishing the opponent's support actions.

If the enemy tries to wash off her anti-heal or other ailments, they take 300 damage. It's free chip damage.

Upon release, Lazara was a direct response to the "Punish Cleanse" meta. She was a must-have for players struggling against Pepperflame and Erlang Shen defenses.
Her stats have clearly aged. A base power of 1015 is quaint compared to modern heroes pushing 1300+. Her damage ceiling has lowered relative to enemy health pools.

While she isn't the nuker she once was, her "Safe Cleanse" mechanic is timeless. As long as developers release heroes that punish cleansing, Lazara will have a niche. She is excellent for War Attack teams facing specific trap defenses, though she struggles to survive as a defensive tank in the modern era.
Lazara is a Barbarian, which gives her a chance to apply bleed on basic attacks.

If you pulled Lazara, you have a gem. She is a "problem solver." Don't worry about her stats being lower than the newest shiny goblin; her ability to safely remove ailments is invaluable for completing hard map stages and winning raids you shouldn't be able to win. Prioritize leveling her!

Lazara is a situational tool. She shouldn't be on your main defense anymore—she's too soft. However, keep her in your War chest. When you see a heavy ailment defense that relies on punishing cleansers, she is the key to unlocking it. She is likely not worth a second Alpha Aether reset if you have newer options, but she is certainly worth keeping at 85+20.
On release, players were baffled by the "without triggering secondary effects" clause. One user noted,
"I don't understand the first skill not triggering... can't think of anyone off the top of my head that has it."
It took time for the community to realize she was the ultimate counter to the specific "Trap" meta.
Players noted her design looks like a gender-swapped version of a standard Orc brute.
"Nice pics. Can you make him also as he is?"
joked one user regarding fan art.
"Lazara has the best animation out there - smashes them with a baseball,"

Lazara is the Babe Ruth of Empires & Puzzles—a heavy hitter with a specific talent that changed the game when she arrived. While her stats have gathered a bit of dust, her "Safe Cleanse" technique is a fundamental skill that remains relevant. She might not be your everyday starter anymore, but when you need a pinch hitter to clear the bases (and the status ailments), she's the one you call.
A situational MVP who has aged gracefully into a vital utility role.
